kept.text:
Klasik SEO Google'a görünür olmayı sağlıyor fakat AEO  ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity gibi AI'lara görünür olmayı sağlıyor, ilk sırada önerilen sitelerin gelecekte çok değerli olacağına inanıyorum bu doğrultuda aşağıdaki işlemleri kişisel siteme uyguladım.

Cloudflare'in yeni aracı isitaagentready standartlarına kendi sitemi uyumlu hale getirdim.Tüm yapay zekalar için içeriğe tam erişim verdim.

Google AI modunda test ettiğimde websitem, hedeflediğim arama sorgusunda 1. sırada önerildi. Artık google ai modu önerileri nedeniyle kullanıcılar sıralamadaki sitelere pek göz atmıyor. 

PageSpeed takıntım nedeniyle  isitaagentready içinde tüm değerleri 100 yapana kadar saatlerce çalıştım. Hali hazırda sitemin performansı oldukça yüksek olduğu için önceki uğraşlarıma nazaran daha kolay oldu sizde kendi sitenizin testlerini henüz yapmadıysanız aracı yorumlar kısmında paylaşıyorum.

kept.text:
整理一份OPC SaaS 出海技术栈清单
1.基础设施
- 服务器：AWS EC2 
- CDN：Cloudflare      
- 域名： Namecheap 
- DNS：Cloudflare       
- OSS / 对象存储：AWS S3 / Cloudflare R2 / 
2.支付（要尽早准备，注册、主体、税务和合规会比较麻烦）
- Stripe
- Paddle / Lemon Squeezy 可作为补充选择
3. 后端    
- NodeJS Express
- Django     
- SpringBoot3
4. 前端
- React       
- Next.js       
- Tailwind CSS       
- shadcn/ui
- 如果需要后台管理，优先用现成组件库，不要一开始重造设计系统
5.数据库
- PostgreSQL
- MySQL       
- Redis       
- Supabase       
- 小团队默认优先选托管数据库，少碰自运维    

OPC 的原则不是堆工具，而是：能上线、能收钱、能稳定维护。
以上仅代表个人观点，不一定是最优解

author.name: Rohan Mukherjee
author.description: Endlessly refactoring and rewriting code at @Cloudflare | Ex-Founding Engineer at @dyte_io (YC W'21) 💻

quoted_tweet.text:
We’re announcing: VibeBench, a new benchmark for what actually matters — how models feel when used on real work by experienced software engineers.

But, we need your help. Here’s how it works:
 
1. An initial cohort of 1000 qualified software engineers (join: https://t.co/07nrXfMthQ)
 
2. Groups of 250 evaluate new models for 2 days on real work.
 
3. Participants subjectively rank the model relative to other models they have experience with.
 
4. On day 4 a report is released with objective results derived from the subjective tests.
 
 How can you help:
 
1. We all need this benchmark to exist, but for it to become reality, we need an initial cohort of 1000 qualified software engineers. If that’s you, please join!

https://t.co/07nrXfMthQ

2. Repost this! We need to reach as many qualified engineers as we can find.

3. Share this initiative with everyone on your engineering teams. Together we can make this benchmark a reality for all of us.

kept.text:
What do OpenAI, Anthropic, Tesla, Cloudflare, Meta, Canva, Shopify, Harvey, & Ramp all have in common? They're all built on @ClickHouseDB.

Here's the simplest way I can explain what ClickHouse does: imagine asking a question about every transaction your company has ever processed and getting the answer before you finish reading this sentence. That's ClickHouse.

I sat down with CEO @ceo_clickhouse and VP of Product @tbragin to tell the ClickHouse story... because once you understand what they've built, the opportunity becomes impossible to ignore. I believe this is a generational company that will (continue to) power the leading AI businesses.

My biggest takeaway from my conversation w/ Aaron & Tanya:

Every company in the world is rearchitecting around AI. But ClickHouse is what AI is architecting around.

As Aaron put it: "Most infrastructure companies were designed before the emergence of agentic systems. These legacy platforms simply weren't designed to meet the volume, concurrency, and dynamic load these AI workloads demand. ClickHouse was designed for this moment."

I've been an investor since the Series C, and I wanted to write a piece that explains the massive opportunity ahead — and what they've actually built — in plain English.

In this piece, I'll cover how:
→ ClickHouse built the world's fastest analytical database — milliseconds where it used to take hours, at 50-70% lower cost than the tools it replaces.
→ ClickHouse is consolidating the entire data stack — replacing five or more separate vendors: Snowflake, Datadog, Elasticsearch, Pinecone, and Postgres.
→ ClickHouse has built the "Agentic Data Stack" — connecting AI agents directly to real-time data so teams can query, reason, and act instantly.
→ ClickHouse has a credible path to $100Billion+ — with a trillion-dollar addressable market and a consumption model that scales with every agent deployed. There's a reason ARR has grown 250% YoY with no SDRs, and enterprise customers have grown from 3,000 to 4,000 in just three months 🤯

Thanks for letting me tell this story, Aaron & Tanya. 

Let's Click into it…

因A社封锁和降智，现在用Codex越来越多，经常有超预期表现。

比如昨天想给博客加个一键发布公众号功能。

Cloudflare部署没有固定IP，无法加公众号白名单，我给它一台VPS的SSH账号，它自己登录写了一个桥接脚本。

带我做了域名解析，写了封面图压缩，终于能从博客发布到公众号草稿箱了。 https://t.co/ZG9Idn8Qib

author.description: 喜欢摇滚乐、爱钓鱼的PM

kept.text:
I just added a fun little toy to our site.

Any time a machine (VM or bare metal) gets provisioned, it shows up as a live notification. Think hotel check-ins, but real, not the fake 'last room available' stuff.

The frontend is fully open source and runs on Cloudflare Workers + Durable Objects.

Our internal backend POSTs an event → Worker → Durable Object → broadcast.

Your browser keeps an open WebSocket to Cloudflare and renders events as they come in. Notifications disappear automatically after a few seconds.

No infra to manage. It just works. Thanks CF!

The others are public: 
- 8.8.8.8 = Google DNS
- 1.1.1.1 = Cloudflare DNS 
- 54.23.12.1 = AWS

author.description: Making the stuff I wished existed.

quoted_tweet.text:
Pull requests disappeared on GitHub for many (all?) users.

This is just the latest outage on a platform where reliability has been beyond unacceptable the last few months.

A fair question: at what point would customers move? How much pain is too much? And where do they move?

author.name: Gergely Orosz
author.description: Writing @Pragmatic_Eng, the #1 software engineering newsletter on Substack. Author of @EngGuidebook. Formerly Uber & Skype.

kept.text: The first quarter of 2026 was a busy one for significant #Internet shutdowns, outages, and other disruptions, and @CloudflareRadar has a unique perspective on their impact. Our latest blog post digs into these events globally. Learn more at https://t.co/CwgQqhu0t2

author.name: Cloudflare Radar
author.description: Internet trends, as seen by the @Cloudflare global network.

value.string_value: The first quarter of 2026 saw a surge in Internet disruptions, from nationwide shutdowns in Uganda and Iran to unprecedented drone strikes on cloud infrastructure. We explore the data behind these...
Shutdowns, power outages, and conflict: a review of Q1 2026 Internet disruptions

kept.text: The vast majority of software stocks have been hit hard due to AI threats to their margins. At some point, we will likely see some divergences and a select few starting to push higher. Cybersecurity stocks like $NET and $CRWD are early contenders. https://t.co/R5bLk4M1Lv
